channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Top House Dem admits Biden debate was a 'setback,' calls for a comeback after 'underwhelming performance'
item.description: A top House Democrat admitted that President Biden did not deliver a great performance at the first presidential debate, as concerns over his candidacy persist.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AOC threatens Supreme Court articles of impeachment over immunity ruling
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez claimed the Supreme Court has been "consumed by a corruption crisis," and vowed to file impeachment articles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden campaign's reset after disastrous debate looks a lot like business as usual
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden's reset after his disastrous debate performance is looking more like a return to business as usual.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP Senate candidate ties opponent to Biden debate: Bob Casey knew
item.description: Vulnerable Democrats are facing new scrutiny over their comments and defense of President Biden's mental acuity and ability to be president for another term after a decidedly lacking debate performance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer insists Biden can win her state and says she's behind him 100%
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer on Monday insisted that President Joe Biden can still win her state, a key battleground in the 2024 election, even as many national Democrats question whether Biden should move forward after his shaky debate performance.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden camp dismisses Trump immunity ruling: 'Doesn't change the facts'
item.description: The Biden campaign blasted former President Trump after the Supreme Court’s ruling granting him immunity on official acts while president, saying the presumptive Republican nominee “thinks he’s above the law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sen. Nicole Mitchell seeks dismissal of burglary charge while calling for release of more evidence
item.description: DFL Sen. Nicole Mitchell of Woodbury was arrested and charged with burglary in April when she was found in the basement of her stepmother’s home in Detroit Lakes. Her next court hearing is now scheduled for July 9.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Could Democrats replace Biden as their nominee? Here's how it could happen, and why it's unlikely
item.description: As President Joe Biden attempts to reassure supporters following his widely panned debate performance on Thursday, Democrats still worried about having him at the top of the ticket in November have few realistic options at their disposal, at least as far as party rules and historical precedent are c
item.dc:creator: LEAH ASKARINAM and ROBERT YOON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats rage at Supreme Court for Trump immunity ruling: 'a travesty'
item.description: Capitol Hill Democrats are hammering the Supreme Court on Monday over its decision to grant immunity to presidents for “official” acts, saying it will empower future commanders in chief to break the law with impunity.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Sotomayor scolds immunity decision for making presidents ‘king above the law’
item.description: Justice Sonia Sotomayor said the Supreme Court’s decision granting former President Trump immunity for official acts “completely insulate[s] presidents from criminal liability” in a forceful dissent issued Monday.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Analysts on presidential debate and recent Supreme Court moves
item.description: MPR News host Angela Davis talks with legal and political analysts about the fallout from Thursday’s presidential debate and the significance of the newest Supreme Court decisions.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis, MPR News

item.title: House Republicans sue Attorney General Garland over access to Biden special counsel interview audio
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Republicans on Monday filed a lawsuit against Attorney General Merrick Garland for the audio recording of President Joe Biden's interview with a special counsel in his classified documents case, asking the courts to enforce their subpoena and reject the White House's effort t
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Schumer: 'Disgraceful' Trump immunity decision will 'weaken' democracy
item.description: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) slammed the Supreme Court’s “disgraceful” decision Monday to shield former President Trump’s official acts from prosecution, which will delay his trial on Jan. 6-related charges.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Trump has presumptive immunity for pressuring Pence to overturn 2020 election
item.description: Former President Trump has presumptive immunity for pressuring then-Vice President Mike Pence to subvert the results of the presidential election in his favor by certifying slates of so-called “fake electors” on Jan. 6, 2021, the Supreme Court ruled Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: US military heightens the security alert at European bases due to a combination of threats
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. military has raised the security protection measures it is taking at its bases throughout Europe, asking service members to be more vigilant and keep a lower profile due to a combination of threats it is seeing across the region. U.S.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Republicans sue Attorney General Garland over access to Biden special counsel interview audio
item.description: House Republicans have filed a lawsuit against Attorney General Merrick Garland for the audio recording of President Joe Biden’s interview with a special counsel in his classified documents case.
item.dc:creator: Farnoush Amiri, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Supreme Court rules ex-presidents have some immunity, sends Trump case back to lower court
item.description: In a historic 6-3 ruling, the justices said for the first time that former presidents have absolute immunity from prosecution for their official acts and no immunity for unofficial acts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: SCOTUS sends Trump immunity case back to lower court but issues ruling on presidential immunity
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday extended the delay in the Washington criminal case against Donald Trump on charges he plotted to overturn his 2020 presidential election loss, all but ending prospects the former president could be tried before the November election.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court sends Trump immunity case back to lower court, dimming chance of trial before election
item.description: The Supreme Court has extended the delay in the Washington criminal case against Donald Trump on charges he plotted to overturn his 2020 presidential election loss, all but ending prospects he could be tried before the November election.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: How the Summer EBT program will help struggling NY families
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York State began accepting applications Monday for a new program designed to help families with school-aged children buy groceries this summer. Families in the Summer EBT program will receive a one-time installment of $120 per child for the summer.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Live updates: Supreme Court rules Trump has presumptive criminal immunity for official acts
item.description: In a monumental decision Monday, the Supreme Court ruled that former presidents enjoy a presumption of criminal immunity for official acts while in the White House, handing a win to former President Trump. The move is likely to delay Trump's federal election subversion case in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5

item.title: Mayor Adams on how $112.4 billion budget will shape future of NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Monday is the first day of the new fiscal year for New York City and the ink is still drying on the record-breaking $112.4 billion budget deal signed by the City Council.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Some student loan borrowers have July payment waived: Here's why
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Millions of student loan borrowers will not have to make debt payments this month. About 3 million borrowers will have both their July payment and interest charges waived.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Republican candidates are downplaying abortion, but it keeps coming up
item.description: Torn between a base that wants more restrictions on abortion and a moderate majority that does not, it seems many Republicans would rather avoid the topic. But they can’t escape talking about it.
item.dc:creator: Julie Rovner, MPR News

item.title: Trump immunity decision in balance as Supreme Court caps term
item.description: The Supreme Court is set to rule on Monday whether former President Trump has immunity from criminal prosecution, a monumental decision that comes just days after the court dropped a decision throwing into doubt charges against hundreds of Jan. 6 rioters and Trump himself.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here's why it would be tough for Democrats to replace Joe Biden on the presidential ticket
item.description: It would be nearly impossible for Democrats to replace President Joe Biden as their 2024 presidential nominee unless he chooses to step aside following his halting debate performance against ex-President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Biden to discuss future of re-election campaign with family on Sunday
item.description: Biden’s trip to Camp David was planned before Thursday’s debate. He and first lady Jill Biden are scheduled to join their children and grandchildren there late Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Carol E. Lee, Kristen Welker, Jonathan Allen, Mike Memoli and Monica Alba, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Poll: Could Gavin Newsom beat Donald Trump?
item.description: California Governor Gavin Newsom, acting as a surrogate for President Biden following Thursday night's debate, once again deflected questions over whether he might potentially replace Biden on the Democratic ticket.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, Fox 5

item.title: How replacing Biden as Democratic nominee would actually work
item.description: Replacing President Biden as the Democratic nominee would be highly complicated, and likely impossible, unless the president voluntarily decided to back out on his own.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court obstruction ruling impacts Tampa Bay Capitol rioters
item.description: The Tampa Bay area has the most Jan. 6 rioters charged per capita in the United States, and some may be impacted by Friday's U.S. Supreme Court ruling about an obstruction-related charge.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NYT editorial board calls on Biden to drop out
item.description: His performance sparked widespread fear among Democrats, and reignited the conversation about whether Biden should step down and allow someone else to take on Trump.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: CNN poll: Most viewers say Trump won debate
item.description: A CNN poll of those who watched the debate late Thursday found 67 percent said former President Donald Trump won the debate, compared to 33 percent who said President Joe Biden won.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WGN 9

item.title: Did Trump actually call veterans who died in wars 'suckers'?
item.description: Near the end of the first 30 minutes, Biden claimed Trump previously verbally attacked and disrespected American veterans, particularly ones who were injured or died during combat.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, WGN 9

item.title: 5 takeaways on a pivotal Trump-Biden debate
item.description: President Biden and former President Trump met for the first time in four years on a debate stage in Atlanta on Thursday in what was cast as a pivotal moment in the presidential race.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, WGN 9

item.title: Trump and Biden mix it up over policy and each other in a debate that turns deeply personal
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — They passed on a handshake at the start, and from there President Joe Biden and former President Donald Trump went right to mixing it up on policy — and each other — in their first 2024 presidential debate on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: How the CNN mute button will work at tonight's Biden-Trump debate
item.description: The mute button is intended to help viewers understand the candidates, but it also carries risks for CNN.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, WGN 9

item.title: Connecticut Gov. Ned Lamont tests positive for COVID-19
item.description: HARTFORD, Conn. (PIX11) -- Connecticut Gov. Ned Lamont has tested positive for COVID-19, a spokesperson said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11
